items.headline: Gold Rises as Traders Weigh Renewed Push for US-Iran Talks
items.summary: Gold climbed after a two-day decline, as renewed optimism for a negotiated end to the US-Iran war eased concerns around inflation.

items.headline: Stocks Rise, Oil Prices Slide on Peace Push Hopes: Markets Wrap
items.summary: US stocks opened higher, with the Nasdaq 100 on pace for its longest streak of gains since 2021, as optimism that the US and Iran are considering another round of peace talks pushed down oil prices and boosted risk appetite around the globe.

items.headline: Wall Street Balances Growth and Uncertainty
items.summary: Big banks are sending mixed signals: trading desks are booming, dealmaking is back, and private credit risks look contained for now. But beneath the strong earnings, CEOs like Jamie Dimon are still warning about an uncertain economy. Bloomberg News reporter Katherine Doherty discussed recent earnings reports from major banks including JPMorgan, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Lucid Names CEO as Saudi PIF, Uber Commit Another $750 Million
items.summary: Lucid Group Inc. named a new chief executive officer and announced $750 million of fresh investment from its two biggest backers as the electric-car maker looks to shore up operations at a crucial juncture.

items.headline: Ship Testing Hormuz Blockade Appears to Transit Then U-Turn
items.summary: A US-sanctioned tanker linked to China that was being watched as one of the first vessels to test President Donald Trump’s naval blockade appeared to U-turn after sailing out of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Herbalife Revives Junk-Debt Sale After Shelving Deal Last Month
items.summary: Herbalife Ltd. is taking another stab at selling junk debt, a month after shelving a loan offering due to market volatility.

items.headline: I Was ‘Madame Due Process,’ Ex-OPEC Head Says At Bribery Trial
items.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke said she sought to increase transparency and competition in Nigeria’s oil industry while she was minister overseeing the sector at a London trial where she faces criminal charges of bribery.

items.headline: BlackRock Sees Private Credit Tumult as Way to Take Market Share
items.summary: BlackRock Inc. Chief Executive Officer Larry Fink sees increased demand for private credit from big institutional investors like insurers, even as retail clients grow skittish over the asset class and seek to redeem more of their shares.

items.headline: Three Ways the Economy Is Like the Mid-Aughts
items.summary: Today’s economy may remind you of the 1970s, but Bloomberg Opinion columnist Kathryn Anne Edwards explains why it’s more like the mid-aughts. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: Colombia Credit Chief Eyes $4 Billion Buyback of External Bonds
items.summary: Colombia is planning to repurchase some $4 billion of its bonds, according to public credit director Javier Cuellar, its third buyback of hard-currency notes in the past year. The bonds are outperforming all their emerging-market peers on Tuesday.

items.headline: Warsh Fed Chair Hearing Set for Next Week, Tim Scott Says
items.summary: The Senate Banking Committee will hold a confirmation hearing next week for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, the panel’s top Republican said Tuesday.

items.headline: Adnoc in Advanced Talks to Buy Shell’s South Africa Gas Stations
items.summary: Shell Plc is in advanced talks with Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. to sell its retail fuel stations in South Africa, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

items.headline: Russia Struggles to Boost Oil Flows After Black Sea Port Hobbled
items.summary: Another key site halted by drone strikes just as Baltic terminals ramp up

items.headline: US Wholesale Prices Rise Less Than Forecast in March
items.summary: US wholesale prices rose by less than expected in March, as the producer price index increased 0.5% after a revised 0.5% rise in February. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: IMF Cut Global Growth to 3.1%, Flags Downturn Risk If War Lasts
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund downgraded its growth projection for the year after the war in the Middle East triggered a major oil shock and included the possibility of a downturn if the conflict drags on and energy infrastructure is severally damaged.

items.headline: TCW Private Credit Fund Slashes Red Lobster Equity Value by 98%
items.summary: TCW Group has marked down its equity stake in Red Lobster by roughly 98% since acquiring it via the restaurant’s 2024 bankruptcy, leaving shares held by a private credit fund it oversees now worth less than $1 million, according to a recent regulatory filing.

items.headline: Japan Exchanges Eye Call-Option ETFs to Attract Global Investors
items.summary: Japan’s two major exchanges are looking to expand exchange-traded fund products including ones that offer covered calls to attract new investors.

items.headline: Stock Futures Extend Gains After Cooler Producer Price Data
items.summary: Stock futures extended gains Tuesday morning after US wholesale prices rose less than expected in March despite a rise in energy prices resulting from the Iran war.

items.headline: Oil Slips as US and Iran Weigh More Talks With Blockade in Place
items.summary: Oil fell on signs Washington and Tehran may revive talks following the start of a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, with traders tracking a China-linked vessel that may test the new curbs.

items.headline: BlackRock Pulls In $130 Billion of Client Cash as Fees Boom
items.summary: BlackRock Inc. took in a net $130 billion of client cash in the first quarter as its focus on higher-fee products helped deliver earnings that beat estimates.

items.headline: Citigroup Reports $22 Billion of Private-Credit Exposure
items.summary: Citigroup Inc. said its exposure to private-credit firms was $22 billion in the first quarter with zero losses, offering a look at what has become a closely watched development by investors.

items.headline: Carlyle Draws $1.5 Billion for Asset-Backed Fund in First Round
items.summary: Carlyle Group Inc. raised $1.5 billion in its first round of fundraising for a new vehicle investing in asset-backed finance as the private equity giant grows its credit business.

items.headline: Citigroup Posts Highest Quarterly Revenue in a Decade
items.summary: Citigroup traders pushed the bank to its highest quarterly revenue in a decade, with the fixed-income unit generating $5.2 billion of revenue in the first quarter. Dani Burger reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.headline: UK Pays Highest Yield for 10-Year Gilt Sale Since 2008
items.summary: The UK sold 10-year bonds at the highest yield since the global financial crisis, drawing record numbers of buyers keen to lock in returns that may fall back if the Middle East war comes to an end.

items.headline: Dollar and VIX Are Back in Tandem as Iran War Usurps Tariff Bets
items.summary: The dollar’s traditional relationship with equity market volatility has been revived by the war in Iran, a signal that haven-seeking investors are finding safety in the US assets they snubbed after last year’s tariff turmoil.

items.headline: Iran Weighs Pausing Hormuz Shipping to Avoid Derailing Talks
items.summary: Iran is considering a short-term pause to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz to avoid testing a US blockade and scuppering a fresh round of peace talks, according to a person familiar with Tehran’s deliberations.

items.headline: Citi Logs Best Returns in Five Years as Revamp Takes Hold
items.summary: Citigroup Inc. traders rode a wave of volatility to push the Wall Street bank to its highest quarterly revenue in a decade, notching another success for Chief Executive Officer Jane Fraser’s turnaround plan.

items.headline: Iran War Lifts Africa Debt Costs That Have Surged Since Pandemic
items.summary: The Iran war is raising borrowing costs for African countries that are only just recovering from the post-pandemic shock that sent debt burdens soaring.

items.headline: EU to Bet on Electricification to Avert Recurring Energy Crises
items.summary: The European Union will float a series of measures next week to help the continent deal with the Iran war’s energy price fallout, as well as electrify the economy to avert future fossil fuel shocks.

items.headline: KKR Limits Redemptions From Asset-Based Fund After 7.2% Sought Withdrawals
items.summary: KKR & Co. has capped withdrawals at 5% of its asset-based finance fund K-ABF, after the vehicle saw redemption requests of roughly 7.2%.

events.event_name: U.S. dollar seen strengthening as U.S.-Israel strikes intensify
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as US war on Iran disrupts shipments
